column2: // Hi-Z (hierarchical depth) pyramid build (review 4.9 step 3, single-phase occlusion groundwork).
// One module, two compute pipelines via the isReduce specialization constant (mirrors cull.comp's
// useMeshShader): reduce builds mip 0 from this view's MSAA camera depth; downsample builds mip
// (srcMip+1) from mip srcMip. Per-mip descriptor sets bind the single destination mip as uMip, so
// no storage-image-array dynamic indexing (an unenabled 1.0 feature) is required. R32F pyramid.
layout(set = 0, binding = 0) uniform sampler2D   uPyramid;            // downsample source (all mips)
layout(set = 0, binding = 1, r32f) writeonly uniform image2D uMip;    // this mip's destination
layout(set = 0, binding = 2) uniform sampler2DMS uDepth;              // reduce source (MSAA depth)
int   srcMip;     // mip to read for downsample (ignored by reduce; reduce reads full-res depth)
ivec2 dstSize;    // destination mip dimensions, px
ivec2 srcSize;    // source dimensions, px: full depth for reduce, mip srcMip for downsample
// Conservative MAX over the source footprint this destination texel covers. Standard ZO depth
// (near=0, far=1, no reverse-Z): the pyramid stores the FARTHEST depth in each texel, so the cull
// rejects an entity only when its nearest point is behind every covered surface. When a source
// dimension is odd the footprint extends one texel at the boundary so no far sample is dropped ���
// dropping one would shrink the stored max and risk culling visible geometry (over-occlusion).
if ((pc.srcSize.x & 1) != 0 && d.x == pc.dstSize.x - 1) x1 = min(x0 + 2, pc.srcSize.x - 1);
if ((pc.srcSize.y & 1) != 0 && d.y == pc.dstSize.y - 1) y1 = min(y0 + 2, pc.srcSize.y - 1);
maxd = max(maxd, texelFetch(uDepth, ivec2(x, y), s).r);
maxd = max(maxd, texelFetch(uPyramid, ivec2(x, y), pc.srcMip).r);
